correctness.notes: Proposition 3 / kink non-Gaussian clause: The reproduced kink limit is (min_{j∈A_L^*} ∇v_j^L(η,λ^*)^T Z_η, max_{j∈A_U^*} ∇v_j^U(η,λ^*)^T Z_η). The note adds that this law is non-Gaussian whenever active branches have distinct gradients, but the missing condition is variance in the induced linear-form differences: for two gradients a,b one needs (a-b)^T Σ_η (a-b)>0. Since Σ_η is singular on the cell-probability simplex, distinct ambient gradients can differ only in a covariance-null direction, e.g. proportional to (1,1,1,1,0,0,0,0), giving (a-b)^T Z_η=0 a.s.; Theorem 2 / first maximizing-cell threshold rule: The reproduced endpoint LP gives finite rational basis values, but the note's threshold rule assumes the cleared equalities v_B^U(P,λ)=v_{B'}^U(P,λ) have a finite positive root set. In degenerate cases two bases can have identical value functions or tied active values over an interval, so the equality polynomial is identically zero and there is no smallest positive feasible root without quotienting identical branches or imposing a tie-breaking/genericity convention.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Proposition 3 / kink non-Gaussian clause: The reproduced kink limit is (min_{j∈A_L^*} ∇v_j^L(η,λ^*)^T Z_η, max_{j∈A_U^*} ∇v_j^U(η,λ^*)^T Z_η). The note adds that this law is non-Gaussian whenever active branches have distinct gradients, but the missing condition is variance in the induced linear-form differences: for two gradients a,b one needs (a-b)^T Σ_η (a-b)>0. Since Σ_η is singular on the cell-probability simplex, distinct ambient gradients can differ only in a covariance-null direction, e.g. proportional to (1,1,1,1,0,0,0,0), giving (a-b)^T Z_η=0 a.s. → The stated sufficient condition for non-Gaussian kink behavior is false; the limit formula and coverage proof survive, but the theorem needs the stronger induced-gradient condition or must weaken the non-Gaussian claim.

perItemFindings.label: vertex
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 2 / first maximizing-cell threshold rule: The reproduced endpoint LP gives finite rational basis values, but the note's threshold rule assumes the cleared equalities v_B^U(P,λ)=v_{B'}^U(P,λ) have a finite positive root set. In degenerate cases two bases can have identical value functions or tied active values over an interval, so the equality polynomial is identically zero and there is no smallest positive feasible root without quotienting identical branches or imposing a tie-breaking/genericity convention. → The endpoint formula remains valid, but the claimed closed-form λ^* rule is not well-defined in tied-basis regimes; the theorem needs a degeneracy convention or a genericity hypothesis.

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[serious, conjecture:inference] Proposition 3 / kink non-Gaussian clause: The reproduced kink limit is (min_{j∈A_L^*} ∇v_j^L(η,λ^*)^T Z_η, max_{j∈A_U^*} ∇v_j^U(η,λ^*)^T Z_η). The note adds that this law is non-Gaussian whenever active branches have distinct gradients, but the missing condition is variance in the induced linear-form differences: for two gradients a,b one needs (a-b)^T Σ_η (a-b)>0. Since Σ_η is singular on the cell-probability simplex, distinct ambient gradients can differ only in a covariance-null direction, e.g. proportional to (1,1,1,1,0,0,0,0), giving (a-b)^T Z_η=0 a.s.. Fix: The stated sufficient condition for non-Gaussian kink behavior is false; the limit formula and coverage proof survive, but the theorem needs the stronger induced-gradient condition or must weaken the non-Gaussian claim.
[serious, conjecture:vertex] Theorem 2 / first maximizing-cell threshold rule: The reproduced endpoint LP gives finite rational basis values, but the note's threshold rule assumes the cleared equalities v_B^U(P,λ)=v_{B'}^U(P,λ) have a finite positive root set. In degenerate cases two bases can have identical value functions or tied active values over an interval, so the equality polynomial is identically zero and there is no smallest positive feasible root without quotienting identical branches or imposing a tie-breaking/genericity convention.. Fix: The endpoint formula remains valid, but the claimed closed-form λ^* rule is not well-defined in tied-basis regimes; the theorem needs a degeneracy convention or a genericity hypothesis.

Referee notes: I reproduced the exclusion collapse, compactness/monotonicity, the nondecomposition counterexample, the response-type LP reduction, and the active-branch inference limit. The main identification/rate term lists match, but two theorem-level degeneracy claims are overstrong and need added tie/covariance qualifications.
